factors.name: US Dollar weakness (DXY)
factors.summary: USD retreating sharply across multiple pairs (EUR/USD toward 1.1645, GBP/USD toward 1.3500, AUD/USD at 0.7167) on geopolitical de-escalation optimism. Weaker USD is single largest structural bullish driver for gold.

factors.name: Real yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS data in news; however, expectations for potential Hormuz reopening and oil price collapse ($90–$95 WTI/Brent) suggest inflation risks subsiding. Real yields likely stable to slightly lower, mildly supportive.

factors.name: Oil prices / inflation expectations
factors.summary: Crude oil fell 6–7% on Monday (WTI near $90/bbl, down from >$100) on hopes of Hormuz reopening and 30-day ceasefire extension. Lower energy inflation reduces Fed inflation-fight urgency, easing pressure on real rates.

factors.name: Geopolitical de-escalation (US-Iran)
factors.summary: Trump administration and Iran negotiating ceasefire extension (60 days), Strait reopening in ~30 days, and uranium enrichment compromise. Risk-off pressure lifted; risk-on flows favor commodities and weaken safe-haven USD.

factors.name: Risk sentiment (equities, commodities, carry)
factors.summary: Equity futures surging to all-time highs on Hormuz peace optimism. AUD/USD, commodity-linked currencies rallying. Risk-on environment typically benefits gold via weaker USD more than it hurts gold via safe-haven flows in this scenario.

factors.name: Fed policy / rate expectations
factors.summary: No new FOMC decision in window; however, Bloomberg reports Treasury curve flashing 'higher-for-longer' warning under new Fed chair Warsh. Suggests markets pricing in sustained higher rates, which would normally pressure gold, but is being overwhelmed by USD weakness.

factors.name: Recent gold price action
factors.summary: Gold up ~1.3–1.4% on Monday alone, bouncing from $4,519 lows to $4,570+. Move is fresh but now 3+ hours in; no major intraday pullback yet reported.

factors.name: Underlying geopolitical risk (Israel-Lebanon, Russia-Ukraine)
factors.summary: Despite US-Iran optimism, Israel intensifying strikes on Hezbollah and Lebanon, Russia threatening Kyiv. These cross-currents provide mild safe-haven bid but are being subordinated to Hormuz deal narrative.

factors.name: Gold ETF flows / institutional positioning
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU flow data in news window; positioning unknown. However, intra-day rally suggests some momentum buying into weakness.

factors.name: Competing safe havens
factors.summary: USD weakness implies yen also weakening (USD/JPY retreating from 159.00); JPY strength from intervention fears might compete for safe-haven flows, but net effect is dollar weakness = gold bullish.

screening.narrative: Gold rallied 1.3–1.4% on Monday as hopes for a US-Iran ceasefire extension and Strait of Hormuz reopening (~30 days) collapsed oil prices 6–7% and sent the US Dollar sharply lower across all major pairs. The geopolitical de-escalation narrative is the dominant driver: lower oil implies lower inflation expectations, reducing Fed pressure, while weaker USD mechanically lifts gold. Risk-on sentiment (equities at all-time highs) is reinforcing commodity strength over safe-haven demand. However, the move is now in Phase 2 (underway ~3+ hours), and no pullback has occurred; confidence is capped by the lack of confirmed retest or reversal structure.
catalysts_ahead: US Consumer Confidence (CB) due Tuesday (May 26) — economic strength reading could stabilize USD
Earnings releases Tuesday (Elbit Systems, Zscaler, Semtech) — limited macro impact
Continued US-Iran negotiations (next 24–72h) — any negative headlines would reverse move sharply
Israel-Lebanon escalation risk — if widens, could trigger risk-off and safe-haven gold bid
Fed speakers (Powell, Waller, Williams) — any hawkish surprise could crush gold rally
Actual Hormuz mine-clearing progress / 30-day timeline confirmation — key for oil/inflation narrative
screening.risk_warning: The dominant risk is a reversal in the US-Iran narrative. If negotiations stall, fail, or geopolitical violence escalates (Israel-Lebanon, Houthis, etc.), oil will spike back above $100, inflation expectations reset higher, USD will reverse and strengthen, and gold will face sharp downside. Additionally, any hawkish Fed commentary (Warsh 'higher-for-longer' signals) or surprise inflation print would invalidate the lower-real-yields tailwind. Gold is also entering overbought short-term (up 1.4% in one session on thin volume); late-cycle phases often see quick pullbacks before resuming higher.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in progress and not yet 4+ hours extended; consider scale-in on a pullback to $4,550–$4,560 (retest of 50% fib of the move) rather than chasing $4,575 resistance. If $4,519 support is broken on escalation news, the entire thesis fails and gold could crater to $4,450–$4,480.

factors.name: USD Strength (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY holding above 99.00 and firming on safe-haven demand amid Iran deal uncertainty. USD/CHF strengthened to 0.7830 snapping 4-day losses; multiple pairs (GBP/USD, EUR/USD, AUD/USD) all weakening. Strong dollar is directly bearish for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields / Nominal Yields
factors.summary: Treasuries rose across the curve on Monday (Memorial Day) as oil crashed ~7% on Iran deal optimism, reducing inflation near-term concerns. However, BOJ Deputy Governor Himino flagged that markets see long-term rate increases reflecting global inflation worries, keeping real yields supported. Mixed but tilt to rate support/inflation risk = mild headwind for gold.

factors.name: Fed Policy / Expectations
factors.summary: No fresh FOMC or Fed speak in the news window. Market focus on BOJ (rate hike path reaffirmed, Middle East risk caveat). No US inflation data or economic releases in the past 24h to shift Fed expectations materially.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran/Hormuz)
factors.summary: US launched fresh 'self-defense strikes' on southern Iran (missile sites, boats) on Monday evening, yet simultaneous peace talks in Doha and Trump statement that negotiations are 'proceeding nicely' suggest deal confidence. Market interpretation: strikes + talks = de-escalation path, not widening conflict. Risk-off initially priced, but hope for resolution is reducing safe-haven demand for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI/Brent)
factors.summary: WTI plunged ~5-7% on Monday as Iran deal optimism outweighed strikes. Currently trading ~$91.79, down 4.98% per latest report. Lower oil = lower inflation expectations, reducing gold's inflation hedge appeal and supporting USD via lower inflation-driven rate expectations.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Equity futures surged after Iran deal reports; KOSPI hit record high. Risk appetite improving on deal optimism reduces safe-haven flows to gold. AUD/USD, NZD/USD, and other risk-sensitive FX pairs weakening due to USD strength, not equity demand for alternatives.

factors.name: Gold Price Action
factors.summary: Gold slipped below $4,580 during Asian session per FXStreet (03:38 UTC), reversing 'major part' of prior day's move higher. Silver similarly down to ~$76.40. Move underway in bearish direction; multiple sources confirm gold is under selling pressure.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy (BoJ)
factors.summary: BOJ Deputy Governor Himino reaffirmed rate hike path but tied timing to Middle East developments. No immediate hawkish surprise; JPY carry unwind risk remains structural but not acute. USD/JPY near 159.00 but no breakthrough move yet.

factors.name: Inflation Data (Recent)
factors.summary: No recent major CPI/PCE release in the news window. Sri Lanka raised policy rate 100bp citing Middle East oil inflation, but this is backward-looking and not a US inflation surprise. No new US inflation print to trigger gold reaction.

factors.name: Macro Confluence
factors.summary: Strong USD + falling oil + peace deal optimism + equity rallies = structural headwinds for gold. Real yields not falling sharply; nominal yields supported. Safe-haven bid is evaporating as geopolitical risk is being priced as containable.

key_levels.resistance: $4,580 (daily resistance, point of reversal), $4,600 (prior swing high)

screening.narrative: Gold is under renewed selling pressure in Asian trade as USD strengthens on safe-haven demand mixed with deal optimism from simultaneous US-Iran peace talks. While fresh US military strikes initially sparked flight-to-safety, the market is interpreting them alongside Trump's 'proceeding nicely' rhetoric and Rubio's confirmation of Qatar talks as a path to ceasefire, not escalation. Oil has crashed ~5-7% on this view, reducing inflation near-term fears and removing the inflation-hedge bid from gold. With DXY holding above 99.00 and risk appetite recovering (equity futures, KOSPI at record), gold's dual bullish factors (safe-haven + inflation hedge) are both fading.
catalysts_ahead: Iran deal language finalization (Rubio indicated 'a few days' remaining; could be resolved by 27-28 May)
Bank of Korea rate decision (28 May, expected hold but 70%+ forecast hike by Q3)
Any escalation in US-Iran conflict or breakdown in Qatar talks (would reverse bearish bias sharply)
US economic data (no major release imminent in next 24-48h per news; NFP not until early June)
Oil price stabilization — if Brent/WTI bounce on deal delay, could underpin inflation expectations and gold
screening.risk_warning: BIAS FLIP RISK: If Iran deal talks collapse or new military escalation occurs (Israel-Hezbollah, Russia-Ukraine flare-up), or if oil rebounds sharply above $100, gold's safe-haven bid returns immediately and could invalidate bearish setup. Conversely, if real yields drop sharply on Fed dovish pivot or US recession fears, gold could turn bullish regardless of USD strength. Watch for any major US inflation miss or downside NFP ahead of FOMC.
screening.entry_guidance: Gold is in PHASE_2 downtrend (move underway for 3-6h, down ~$15-25 from prior day highs). SELL on retest of $4,580 resistance with risk above $4,600; TARGET $4,550-$4,530 support. For longs, wait for gold to close below $4,550 (potential capitulation spike) or for headlines to flip bearish (deal collapse, oil bounce) before reconsidering BUY.
